quality.rationale: 多步微分形式外微分计算结合分部积分法，逻辑链条清晰且具有一般性；结构直接对应Poincaré引理的构造证明，泛化性强；属纯微分几何推导，缺乏具体天体或工程物理场景。

origin.question:
[文件 1: 1_s8_8.6.md]

Problem 8.6. Consider a 2-form in $n$ -dimensional space:
$$
\alpha = f \left(x^{1}, x^{2}, \dot s , x^{n}\right) \widetilde {\mathrm {d} x^{1}} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d} x^{2}}.
$$
Suppose for some region of space including $\mathbf{x}^1 = 0$ , that
$$
\mathrm {d} \alpha = 0.
$$
Construct the 1-form
$$
\widetilde {\boldsymbol {\beta}} = \left[ \mathrm {x}^{1} \int_{0}^{1} \mathrm {f} (\xi \mathrm {x}^{1}, \mathrm {x}^{2}, \dot s , \mathrm {x}^{\mathrm {n}}) \mathrm {d} \xi \right] \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{2}
$$
and show that $\alpha = \mathrm{d}\tilde{\beta}$
origin.solution:
Solution 8.6. Define
$$
\mathbf {g} \equiv \left[ x^{1} \int_{0}^{1} f \left(\xi x^{1}, x^{2}, \dot s x^{n}\right) d \xi \right],
$$
so that $	ilde{\pmb{\beta}} = \mathbf{g}\widetilde{\mathbf{dx}}^2$ and
$$
\mathrm {d} \widetilde {\boldsymbol {\beta}} = \mathrm {g}_{, i} \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{i} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{2} = \mathrm {g}_{, 1} \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{1} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{2} + \mathrm {g}_{, 3} \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{3} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{2} + \dot s .
$$
Now
$$
\mathbf {g},_{1} = \left[ \int_{0}^{1} f \left(\xi \mathbf {x}^{1}, \mathbf {x}^{2}, \dot s \mathbf {x}^{\mathrm {n}}\right) d \xi \right] + \left[ \mathbf {x}^{1} \int_{0}^{1} \xi f^{\prime} \left(\xi \mathbf {x}^{1}, \mathbf {x}^{2}, \dot s \mathbf {x}^{\mathrm {n}}\right) d \xi \right]
$$
where $f'$ indicates a derivative with respect to the first argument. The second term above can be written as
$$
\begin{array}{l} \int_{0}^{1} \mathrm {x}^{1} \xi \mathrm {f}^{\prime} (\xi \mathrm {x}^{1}, \mathrm {x}^{2} \dot s) \mathrm {d} \xi = \int_{0}^{1} \xi \frac {\partial}{\partial \xi} \mathrm {f} (\xi \mathrm {x}^{1}, \mathrm {x}^{2}, \dot s) \mathrm {d} \xi \\\\= \left. \xi \mathrm {f} (\xi \mathrm {x}^{1}, \mathrm {x}^{2}, \dot s) \right|_{0}^{1} - \int_{0}^{1} \mathrm {f} (\xi \mathrm {x}^{1}, \mathrm {x}^{2}, \dot s) \mathrm {d} \xi , \\\\end{array}
$$
where we have integrated by parts; we have then
$$
g_{, 1} = \xi f (\xi x^{1}, x^{2}, \dot s x^{n}) |_{0}^{1} = f (x^{1}, x^{2}, \dot s x^{n}).
$$
Next, $\mathbf{d}\alpha = 0$ tells us that
$$
\mathbf {d} \alpha = f_{, 3} \mathbf {d x}^{3} \wedge \mathbf {d x}^{1} \wedge \mathbf {d x}^{2} + f_{, 4} \mathbf {d x}^{4} \wedge \mathbf {d x}^{1} \wedge \mathbf {d x}^{2} + \dot s = 0 ,
$$
which can only be true if $f_{,3} = f_{,4} = f_{,5} = \dot s f_{,n} = 0$ , and hence
$$
\mathbf {g}_{, 3} = \left[ \mathbf {x}^{1} \int_{0}^{1} \mathrm {f}_{, 3} \left(\xi \mathbf {x}^{1}, \mathbf {x}^{2}, \dot s , \mathbf {x}^{\mathrm {n}}\right) \mathrm {d} \xi \right] = 0.
$$
Similarly $\mathbf{g}_{,4} = \mathbf{g}_{,5} = \dot s \mathbf{g}_{,n} = 0$ , so that finally
$$
\mathrm {d} \widetilde {\boldsymbol {\beta}} = \mathrm {g}, 1 \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{1} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{2} = \mathrm {f} \left(\mathrm {x}^{1}, \mathrm {x}^{2}, \dot s \mathrm {x}^{\mathrm {n}}\right) \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{1} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{2} = \alpha .
$$
